description: End-to-end pipeline: Claroty asset inventory -> AI SIEM lookup -> Palo Alto traffic/threat logs -> correlation -> alerts -> Hyperautomation tagging. Each row maps to one pipeline stage.

graphs.title: Pipeline Overview
graphs.markdown:
## Claroty x Palo Alto OT Pipeline

**Stage 1.** Claroty asset inventory ingested every cycle to `serverHost=claroty-inventory`.

**Stage 2.** Inventory populates an AI SIEM lookup of `ip -> {hostname, device_type, criticality, claroty_risk, expected_edl, expected_dag, approved_policy_group, status}`.

**Stage 3.** Palo Alto traffic / threat / IP-tag logs land at `serverHost=pan-library-trigger` (and pre-enriched at `pan-claroty-enriched`).

**Stage 4.** Each PAN event is correlated with Claroty context; 5 drift flags are computed at ingest (`claroty_edl_drift_flag`, `claroty_dag_reclassified_flag`, `claroty_unmanaged_cross_zone_flag`, `claroty_highrisk_offzone_flag`, `claroty_decommissioned_active_flag`).

**Stage 5.** Mismatches drive **5 Custom STAR rules** (Claroty drift) and the **7 PANW Library rules** (Library + Purple AI auto-investigation).

**Stage 6.** Approved verdicts feed Hyperautomation workflow `Claroty/PANW Alert — Auto Investigation + Verdict-driven Response`, which updates the PAN EDL / DAG.

graphs.title: PAN events (24h)
graphs.query: dataSource.name = 'Palo Alto Networks Firewall' | group count() | limit 1

graphs.title: Drift events (24h)
graphs.query: serverHost = 'pan-claroty-enriched' (claroty_edl_drift_flag = '1' or claroty_dag_reclassified_flag = '1' or claroty_unmanaged_cross_zone_flag = '1' or claroty_highrisk_offzone_flag = '1' or claroty_decommissioned_active_flag = '1') | group count() | limit 1

graphs.title: Distinct OT src IPs in drift
graphs.query: serverHost = 'pan-claroty-enriched' (claroty_edl_drift_flag = '1' or claroty_dag_reclassified_flag = '1' or claroty_unmanaged_cross_zone_flag = '1' or claroty_highrisk_offzone_flag = '1' or claroty_decommissioned_active_flag = '1') | group estimate_distinct(src.ip.address) | limit 1

graphs.title: Stage 1+2 - Claroty inventory ingest over time
graphs.query: serverHost = 'claroty-inventory' claroty_site = $plant | group count() by timestamp=timebucket('1h'), claroty_site | transpose claroty_site on timestamp

graphs.title: Stage 3 - PAN events ingested over time
graphs.query: dataSource.name = 'Palo Alto Networks Firewall' | group count() by timestamp=timebucket('1h'), serverHost | transpose serverHost on timestamp

graphs.title: Stage 4 - Drift events by flag type
graphs.query: serverHost = 'pan-claroty-enriched' (claroty_edl_drift_flag = '1' or claroty_dag_reclassified_flag = '1' or claroty_unmanaged_cross_zone_flag = '1' or claroty_highrisk_offzone_flag = '1' or claroty_decommissioned_active_flag = '1') | let drift_type = claroty_edl_drift_flag == '1' ? 'EDL Drift' : claroty_dag_reclassified_flag == '1' ? 'DAG Reclassified' : claroty_unmanaged_cross_zone_flag == '1' ? 'Unmanaged Cross-Zone' : claroty_highrisk_offzone_flag == '1' ? 'High-Risk Off-Zone' : claroty_decommissioned_active_flag == '1' ? 'Decommissioned Active' : 'No Flag' | group hits = count() by drift_type | sort -hits

graphs.title: Stage 4 - Drift by Claroty site
graphs.query: serverHost = 'pan-claroty-enriched' (claroty_edl_drift_flag = '1' or claroty_dag_reclassified_flag = '1' or claroty_unmanaged_cross_zone_flag = '1' or claroty_highrisk_offzone_flag = '1' or claroty_decommissioned_active_flag = '1') | group hits = count() by claroty_site | sort -hits

graphs.title: Stage 4 - Top drifting OT assets (24h)
graphs.query: serverHost = 'pan-claroty-enriched' (claroty_edl_drift_flag = '1' or claroty_dag_reclassified_flag = '1' or claroty_unmanaged_cross_zone_flag = '1' or claroty_highrisk_offzone_flag = '1' or claroty_decommissioned_active_flag = '1') | group hits = count(), criticality = any(claroty_criticality), risk = any(claroty_risk), site = any(claroty_site), status = any(claroty_status) by claroty_hostname | sort -hits | limit 20

graphs.title: Stage 5 - Recent Claroty STAR drift events (rule fires)
graphs.query: serverHost = 'pan-claroty-enriched' (claroty_edl_drift_flag = '1' or claroty_dag_reclassified_flag = '1' or claroty_unmanaged_cross_zone_flag = '1' or claroty_highrisk_offzone_flag = '1' or claroty_decommissioned_active_flag = '1') | group hits = count(), criticality = any(claroty_criticality), risk = any(claroty_risk), site = any(claroty_site), status = any(claroty_status), hostname = any(claroty_hostname), expected_edl = any(claroty_expected_edl), observed_edl = any(src_external_dynamic_list), edl_drift = any(claroty_edl_drift_flag), reclassified = any(claroty_dag_reclassified_flag), unmanaged = any(claroty_unmanaged_cross_zone_flag), highrisk = any(claroty_highrisk_offzone_flag), decommissioned = any(claroty_decommissioned_active_flag) by scenario | sort -hits | limit 25

graphs.title: Stage 5 - PANW Library triggers with Claroty enrichment
graphs.query: serverHost = 'pan-library-trigger' | group hits = count(), criticality = any(claroty_criticality), risk = any(claroty_risk), site = any(claroty_site), status = any(claroty_status), hostname = any(claroty_hostname), in_inventory = any(claroty_in_inventory), context = any(claroty_context) by library_rule_target | sort -hits | limit 25

graphs.title: Stage 6 - Hyperautomation runbook reference
graphs.markdown:
### Stage 6 - Hyperautomation `Claroty/PANW Alert - Auto Investigation + Verdict-driven Response`

**Workflow id**: `3129f237-1718-482e-80da-e17a32801654` (state = *active*)

**Trigger filter groups**:

1. STAR alerts: `name contains 'Claroty'` AND `severity in [HIGH, CRITICAL]`
2. Library alerts: `name contains 'PANW Firewall'` OR `name contains 'Palo Alto Networks Firewall'`

**Steps**: capture alert context -> call `S1/aiInvestigation/run` (Library alerts only) -> email *investigation started* -> wait 180 s -> read `aiInvestigation.verdict` -> if `FALSE_POSITIVE` => email + close alert; else => email findings + recommendations.

Approved verdicts should drive **PAN EDL / DAG update** via the `Update-PAN-EDL` action (binding to Palo Alto Networks Firewall integration in *Hyperautomation -> Integrations*).

*Counts shown above use the raw underlying events. For the alert objects themselves and analyst verdicts, switch to Detect -> Findings.*
